# Coding Plan for openclaw/openclaw#60237: [Feature]: Add threadPromptGuideline for customizable auto-generated Discord thread titles

Source report: [records/openclaw-openclaw/items/60237.md](records/openclaw-openclaw/items/60237.md)

## Summary

Keep open. Current main still has only the fixed Discord generated-title prompt and no channel config field, schema entry, runtime plumbing, docs entry, or tests for a custom thread-title guideline; the related merged PR added generated titles but not this customization.

## Plan

Implement https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/60237 as a focused Discord plugin enhancement. Add an optional per-channel `threadPromptGuideline` string for Discord guild channel config, validate it with a 500-character maximum, carry it through allow-list resolution into the generated auto-thread rename path, and pass it to `generateThreadTitle` as bounded operator guidance while preserving the existing concise 3-6 word/title-only constraints and fallback behavior. Update TypeScript config types, zod schema, generated channel config metadata/baselines, Discord docs, focused tests for prompt composition and runtime plumbing, and add a user-facing changelog entry. Do not change the default `autoThreadName` behavior, thread creation timing, model selection, auth handling, or unrelated Discord thread behavior. Validate with focused Discord tests, config docs check, formatting on touched files, and `pnpm check:changed` in Testbox before handoff.
